question: What is a cause of ectopic kidney during fetal development?

options.text: Failure of the kidney to ascend from the pelvis
options.explanation: Ectopic kidney occurs when a developing kidney fails to ascend to its normal position.

options.text: Obstruction of the ureter after birth
options.explanation: Ureteral obstruction does not cause congenital ectopic positioning of the kidney.
Autoimmune renal injury affects function, not embryologic kidney location.

options.text: Trauma causing kidney displacement in adulthood
options.explanation: Adult trauma may displace organs but does not cause congenital ectopic kidney.

question: Which embryologic defect most directly causes coloboma?

options.text: Failure of the optic fissure to close completely
options.explanation: Coloboma results when the optic fissure fails to close during early eye development.

options.text: Failure of the lens placode to invaginate
options.explanation: Lens placode invagination defects cause lens abnormalities, not coloboma.
Persistence of the hyaloid artery is associated with persistent fetal vasculature, not coloboma.

options.text: Abnormal migration of neural crest cells
options.explanation: Neural crest migration defects can affect ocular structures, but they do not specifically cause coloboma.
